RECEIVED
3/27/90

RECEIVED
3/29/90

26 March 90

Diane Griffiths
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices
428 "J" Street, #800
Sacramento, California 95814

Subject: Request for Legal Opinion on Behalf of Santa Clarita
City Council Member Janice H. Heidt

Enclosures: (1) Letter dated 13 March 1990 from Douglas R.
Ring alleging conflict of interest on his clients
projects.
(2) Letters dated 26 August 1988 requesting an
earlier opinion on Westcreek Tract #32365.
(3) Map of Westcreek project Tract #32365.
(4) Map of all four projects that are both
separate and part of a developer agreement.
(5) Notice of determination dated 2 April 1987 from
Los Angeles County Department of Regional Planning.
(6) Letter dated 22 April 1988 to Board of
Supervisors, Los Angeles County.

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

I am a City Council Member for the City of Santa Clarita. In
August 1988, we asked for a legal opinion on a possible conflict
of interest alleged by the Developer of Tract #32365 as indicated
in Enclosure (2). At that time, because we were not certain where
the road (Dockweiler Drive) would enter Sierra Hwy from the tract,
I abstained from the vote and discussion by stating that there may
be a conflict of interest. At no time did I indicate that there was
a conflict of interest.

Since that time, the road (Dockweiler Drive) has been
completed and the condominiums are under construction. Now the
Developer proposes to give to the City of Santa Clarita the
northern corner of Tract #32365 as right-of-way for a road the City
of Santa Clarita wants to build. In exchange, the Developer has
asked that the City fast track three other separate projects and
should they be approved, a Developer Agreement would be considered
to obtain the said right-of-way and other road improvements. Thus,
there are four separate actions to be taken by our City Council.
The three new projects to be considered are three to eight miles
away from the original Tract #32365. One is even outside the city
limits and would be annexed as part of the Developer Agreement.

The hearing on these projects and the developer agreement were
set for 13 March 1990. On 28 February 1990, my City attorney called
to tell me that Douglas R. Ring, attorney for the Developer had
notified him that they felt I had a conflict of interest. I
drafted a letter to the FPPC but decided to call instead as there
was not time to get a formal opinion by 13 March. After several
attempts and messages, I finally got through to one one of your
staff attorneys, a young man, on 8 or 9 March. He indicated to me

that there would probably be no problem with the three individual
projects but the original Tract #32365 would continue to pose the
same problem as it did in 1988. I apologize for not being more
specific as to the date of the conversation and who I talked with.
In any event, when I arrived at the 13 March Council Meeting, I was
given a copy of Enclosure (1), the letter from Douglas Ring. The
council opened the public hearings that evening and then continued
the items after hearing a staff report.

After that meeting, I got to thinking about the original
situation. Back in 1988, we were not sure where the road
(Dockweiler Drive) would enter Sierra Hwy. It seemed wiser to
abstain from the vote than take a chance. Well, the road is now
in and it is the closest point of the project to our optioned
property. So I drove, corner to corner, from the SW corner of
Sierra Hwy and San Fernando Road to the corner of Dockweiler Drive
and Sierra Hwy. Driving it is nearly a straight line. The odometer
showed that it was between .5 and .6 miles or approximately 2904
feet. I convinced my husband that we should go out and measure it
with a wheel. So we did, he went uphill and I went downhill. We
bisected Sierra Hwy on a straight line between the two corners. It
was 2901 feet. Please note the green triangle I have drawn on
Enclosure (3). We know from Enclosure (5) that the project is
located 3/4 of a mile (3960 feet) northwesterly of the intersection
of Sierra Hwy and San Fernando Road and from Enclosure (6) that
Dockweiler Drive is 2500 feet from Sierra Hwy to the project
easterly boundary. By taking the Formula A squared (2901 feet
Dockweiler to our property plus B squared (length of Dockweiler
Drive) equals C squared (the third leg of the triangle), C = 3829.7
feet and pretty darn close to that 3/4 mile that Enclosure (5)
indicated.

That puts our property outside of the 2500 foot limit. My
understanding of law is that if my property is more than 2500 feet,
a conflict would exist if the effect on my property is not the same
as the effect on at least 25% of the properties within 2500 feet
or that there are not at least 10 properties under separate
ownership within 2500 feet. I know that there are more than 10
properties under separate ownership within the 2500 foot radius,
as it points out in Enclosure (6), seven parcels had to be
condemned just to get Dockweiler Drive. There are several that can
be identified including a gas station, two mobile home parks, a
nursery, strip center, another condo project and thats just the San
Fernando Road side. However, I really do not understand clearly
the first part of the test involving 25% of the properties. Could
you please elaborate on that with some examples.

I would appreciate a response as your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,
Janice H. Heidt
Janice H. Heidt
City Council Member
City Of Santa Clarita

ENCLOSURE (1)
GOLD, MARKS, RING & PEPPER
1800 AVENUE OF THE STARS
SUITE 300
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90067
TELEPHONE (213) 277-1000
FACSIMILE (213) 883-4847

March 13, 1990

Mr. John Larson
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Conflict of Interest Opinion

Dear Mr. Larson:

I represent G.H. Palmer Associates, a real estate development company which has applied for various municipal approvals from the City of Santa Clarita.

A Councilmember, together with her husband, own a piece of property which will be economically impacted by the approval or denial of my client's projects. If the projects are approved, her property may be reduced in value. If the projects are denied, her property may be increased in value.

While there may have been informal conversations with staff regarding this issue, no formal opinion letter has been issued. We would appreciate a formal opinion letter as to whether, given the facts described, the Councilmember may participate in the vote.

If additional facts or information are necessary, please do not hesitate to let me know.

Thanking you in advance for your cooperation.

Very truly yours,

Douglas R. Ring
of GOLD, MARKS, RING & PEPPER

April 22, 1988

IN REPLY PLEASE
REFER TO FILE:

PD-2
815.3

Honorable Board of Supervisors
County of Los Angeles
383 Hall of Administration
500 West Temple Street
Los Angeles, California 90012

ADOPTED

49

MAY 03 1988

Dear Supervisors:

DOCKWEILER DRIVE FROM SIERRA HIGHWAY TO
2500 FEET WESTERLY OF SIERRA HIGHWAY
CITY OF SANTA CLARITA
SUPERVISORIAL DISTRICT 5
4-VOTE MATTER

LARRY J. MONTEITH
EXECUTIVE OFFICER

RECOMMENDATION:

That your Board adopt the attached Resolution in the form pre-
viously approved by County Counsel.

Jurisdiction is requested in order that the County may acquire
right of way on portions of dockweiler Drive from Sierra Highway to
the easterly boundary of Tract 23365 approximately 2500 feet
westerly of Sierra Highway, which is located entirely in the City
of Santa Clarita.

The developer of Tract 32365 is required to construct off-site
improvements on this segment of Dockweiler Drive as a condition of
approval of Tract 32365. Several months ago, the developer
notified the County that he was unable to acquire the necessary
right of way from seven parcels within this segment of Dockweiler
Drive and requested County assistance in acquiring these parcels.
On December 8, 1987, the Board of Supervisors entered into
Agreement 58168 with the developer whereby the County will attempt
to acquire the seven parcels in behalf of the subdivider with the
developer financing all costs associated with the acquisition.

The right of way acquisition is scheduled to begin following the
City's consent to jurisdiction. The County approved the
environmental document for this Tract, including the off-site
improvement, on October 30, 1986 and made a finding that the
project would not have a significant effect on the environment.

(w) 39W28JUI4

Honorable Board of Supervisors
Page 2
April 22, 1988


This jurisdiction will be relinquished after the completion of this
project.

Upon the adoption of this Resolution, please return two certified
copies to the Department of Public Works for transmittal to the
City.

Respectfully submitted,

[signature]
T. A./TIDEMANSON
Director of Public Works

AUG-26-1988 15:05 FROM GERALD H. HEIDT COMPANY TO 1213-6239297 P.02/05

GERALD H. HEIDT COMPANY
COMMERCIAL/INDUSTRIAL DEVELOPMENT AND MANAGEMENT
38480 LYONS AVENUE. • NEWHALL, CALIFORNIA 91321
(818) 867-1842 (805) 255-2200

August 26, 1988

Mr. Carl Newton
City Attorney
City of Santa Clarita
Suite 101
21021 Soledad Canyon Road
Santa Clarita, California 91351

Dear Mr. Newton:

Pursuant to my request for a clarification from the Fair Political
Practices Commission of a possible conflict of interest on Los
Angeles County Tract #32365 - 903 Units, the following information is
submitted.

Attached please find an area map which shows the properties in question. The location labeled "SITE" is Tract 32365. Location (1) is
Lyons Station Shopping Center and One for The Books bookstore. Location (2) is a property under option by the Gerald H. Heidt Company.
Location (3) is Dockweiler Drive.

Tract 32365 - 903 Units is before the Santa Clarita City Council, of
which I am a member, because Los Angeles County has requested jurisdiction from Santa Clarita to acquire right-of-way for Dockweiler
Drive (location 3), a second access to the tract which runs approximately 2500 feet westerly from Sierra Highway to the project.

Three hours prior to Santa Clarita City Council meeting of 25 August
1988, at which Tract 32365 was to be considered, the attorney for the
owner of the tract contacted Santa Clarita City Attorney Carl Newton.
He indicated that there may be a conflict of interest if I discussed
or voted on this agenda item because my husband, Gerald Heidt, had a
property (location 2)under option in the vicinity of Tract 32365. We
also own Lyons Station Shopping Center and a Bookstore in the same
area.

Lyons Station Shopping Center (location 1) is approximately 2 1/2
miles from Tract 32365. It was built in 1978 by the Heidt & Cowgill
Investment Company, an equal general partnership between Gerald H.
Heidt and Robert W. Cowgill. One for The Books bookstore is located
in the center and has been owned since 1982 in sole proprietorship by
Janice H. Heidt and Gerald H. Heidt.

The optioned property (location 2) is approximately 7/8 mile from
Tract 32365 on either San Fernando Road or Sierra Highway. The property
was optioned in March 1988 by the Gerald H. Heidt Company, a sole
proprietorship of which Gerald H. Heidt is sole owner. It is a property that is commercially zoned and would be described in development
jargon as "freeway commercial" because it is adjacent to north and
south bound ramps of the Antelope Valley Freeway (Highway 14). My
husband intends to develop it as a retail center that would cater to
freeway traffic.

AUG-26-1988 16:06 FROM: GERALD H. HEIDT COMPANY TO 1213-6238297 P.01/05

Mr. Carl Newton                                                    Page 2
August 26, 1988


My husband and I are 17 year residents and business owners of the
Santa Clarita Valley. This area has experienced what could only be
described as "explosive growth" over the past few years. Any business
located here would experience growth from the new housing units
already occupied and those on the drawing board. The question for the
Past Politicians Commission to determine is whether this
constitutes a conflict of interest. Since the rate of growth in Santa
Clarita continues unabated, this issue is likely to come up again on
other projects that the city council may consider in the "vicinity".
An objective reading by the FPPC is needed to clarify the situation.


Janice H. Heidt                           Gerald H. Heidt
[signature]                               [signature]
Mayor Pro-Tem                             Owner
City of Santa Clarita                     Gerald H. Heidt Co.

embedding.qa_text: 26 March 90 Diane Griffiths General Counsel Fair Political Practices 428 "J" Street, #800 Sacramento, California 95814 Subject: Request for Legal Opinion on Behalf of Santa Clarita City Council Member Janice H. Heidt Enclosures: (1) Letter dated 13 March 1990 from Douglas R. Ring alleging conflict of interest on his clients projects. (2) Letters dated 26 August 1988 requesting an earlier opinion on Westcreek Tract #32365. (3) Map of Westcreek project Tract #32365. (4) Map of all four projects that are both separate and part of a developer agreement. (5) Notice of determination dated 2 April 1987 from Los Angeles County Department of Regional Planning. (6) Letter dated 22 April 1988 to Board of Supervisors, Los Angeles County. Dear Ms. Griffiths: I am a City Council Member for the City of Santa Clarita. In August 1988, we asked for a legal opinion on a possible conflict of interest alleged by the Developer of Tract #32365 as indicated in Enclosure (2). At that time, because we were not certain where the road (Dockweiler Drive) would enter Sierra Hwy from the tract, I abstained from the vote and discussion by stating that there may be a conflict of interest. At no time did I indicate that there was a conflict of interest. Since that time, the road (Dockweiler Drive) has been completed and the condominiums are under construction. Now the Developer proposes to give to the City of Santa Clarita the northern corner of Tract #32365 as right-of-way for a road the City Of Santa Clarita wants to build. In exchange, the Developer has asked that the City fast track three other separate projects and should they be approved, a Developer Agreement would be considered to obtain the said right-of-way and other road improvements. Thus, there are four separate actions to be taken by our City Council. The three new projects to be considered are three to eight miles away from the original Tract #32365. One is even outside the city limits and would be annexed as part of the Developer Agreement. The hearing on these projects and the developer agreement were set for 13 March 1990. On 28 February 1990, my City attorney called to tell me that Douglas R. Ring, attorney for the Developer had notified him that they felt I had a conflict of interest. I drafted a letter to the FPPC but decided to call instead as there was not time to get a formal opinion by 13 March. After several attempts and messages, I finally got through to one one of your staff attorneys, a young man, on 8 or 9 March. embedding.first_500_words: RECEIVED 3/27/90 RECEIVED 3/29/90 26 March 90 Diane Griffiths General Counsel Fair Political Practices 428 "J" Street, #800 Sacramento, California 95814 Subject: Request for Legal Opinion on Behalf of Santa Clarita City Council Member Janice H. Heidt Enclosures: (1) Letter dated 13 March 1990 from Douglas R. Ring alleging conflict of interest on his clients projects. (2) Letters dated 26 August 1988 requesting an earlier opinion on Westcreek Tract #32365. (3) Map of Westcreek project Tract #32365. (4) Map of all four projects that are both separate and part of a developer agreement. (5) Notice of determination dated 2 April 1987 from Los Angeles County Department of Regional Planning. (6) Letter dated 22 April 1988 to Board of Supervisors, Los Angeles County. Dear Ms. Griffiths: I am a City Council Member for the City of Santa Clarita. In August 1988, we asked for a legal opinion on a possible conflict of interest alleged by the Developer of Tract #32365 as indicated in Enclosure (2). At that time, because we were not certain where the road (Dockweiler Drive) would enter Sierra Hwy from the tract, I abstained from the vote and discussion by stating that there may be a conflict of interest. At no time did I indicate that there was a conflict of interest. Since that time, the road (Dockweiler Drive) has been completed and the condominiums are under construction. Now the Developer proposes to give to the City of Santa Clarita the northern corner of Tract #32365 as right-of-way for a road the City of Santa Clarita wants to build. In exchange, the Developer has asked that the City fast track three other separate projects and should they be approved, a Developer Agreement would be considered to obtain the said right-of-way and other road improvements. Thus, there are four separate actions to be taken by our City Council. The three new projects to be considered are three to eight miles away from the original Tract #32365. One is even outside the city limits and would be annexed as part of the Developer Agreement. The hearing on these projects and the developer agreement were set for 13 March 1990. On 28 February 1990, my City attorney called to tell me that Douglas R. Ring, attorney for the Developer had notified him that they felt I had a conflict of interest. I drafted a letter to the FPPC but decided to call instead as there was not time to get a formal opinion by 13 March. After several attempts and messages, I finally got through to one one of your staff attorneys, a young man, on 8 or 9 March. He indicated to me that there would probably be no problem with the three individual projects but the original Tract #32365 would continue to pose the same problem as it did in 1988. I apologize for not being more specific as to the date of the conversation and who I talked with. In any event, when I arrived at the 13
